title: MD
biography: Adel Mahmoud was an Egyptian-American physician and vaccinologist who led the development of the rotavirus vaccine (RotaTeq) and HPV vaccine (Gardasil) as president of Merck Vaccines. These two vaccines alone have prevented millions of deaths worldwide from cervical cancer and childhood diarrheal disease.
aiSummary: Adel Mahmoud was an infectious disease and vaccinology specialist with an H-index of 65 at Princeton University and Merck. Led development of Gardasil and RotaTeq vaccines. Based in United States.
education: London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine

affiliations.role: Professor of Molecular Biology and Public Policy

citations.title: The vaccine enterprise can and must do better

timeline.title: Chair of Medicine at Case Western Reserve
timeline.description: Led department of medicine while conducting tropical disease research

timeline.title: President of Merck Vaccines
timeline.description: Took leadership of Merck's vaccine division, championing rotavirus and HPV vaccines

timeline.title: Gardasil (HPV vaccine) approved by FDA
timeline.description: First vaccine to prevent cervical cancer received FDA approval under his leadership

timeline.title: Joined Princeton University faculty
timeline.description: Became professor of molecular biology and public policy
